ZBW — Leibniz Information Centre for Economics, Kiel, Germany

titles.title: An Exploration of Scientific Press Releases in the Context of Altmetrics

descriptions.description: This poster explores two large samples of science press releases — 26,358 from EurekAlert! and 1,856 from IDW-Online — published between April 2016 and March 2017, to gain insights on their contents, creators, and role in the communication of research. Content analysis of 100 random samples from each platform (inter-rater κ=0.89) examined coverage of methods, limitations, implications, and references. Both platforms are dominated by life and physical sciences. EurekAlert! is shaped more by publishers/journals; IDW-Online features more non-university research institutes. Future work will apply machine learning to classify larger samples.

Two samples of press releases (April 2016–March 2017): 26,358 from EurekAlert! and 1,856 from IDW-Online. Two randomly chosen subsamples of 100 press releases each examined by two independent raters (Cohen's kappa κ=0.89). Content analysis for: methods, limitations, implications, references, no research. Disciplines determined from Scopus subject classifications of linked journal articles (EurekAlert!) or IDW-assigned categories. Porter Stemming Algorithm used for topic analysis.

fundingReferences.funderName: German Federal Ministry of Education and Research

sections.sectionContent: Press releases are published by organizations to bring attention to achievements. In the scientific domain, research institutions, funders, and academic publishers use press releases to advertise newly published papers. For altmetrics research, press releases have been an almost unregarded but promising source of data (Bowman & Hassan, 2019). Previous studies show a strong association between press release promotion and later citations and altmetrics (Lemke, 2020).

Two samples April 2016–March 2017: 26,358 press releases from EurekAlert! (avg 755 words); 1,856 from IDW-Online (avg 724 words). Subsamples of 100 each analyzed by two independent raters (Cohen's kappa κ=0.89) for: Methods, Limitations, Implications, References, No Research.

EurekAlert! top disciplines: Life Sciences (13.95%), Health Sciences (12.79%), Medicine (12.59%), Biochemistry (9.27%), Physical Sciences (7.55%). IDW-Online top disciplines: Biology (13.67%), Medicine (11.13%), Environment and Ecology (7.76%), Chemistry (6.57%), Physics (6.35%).
Methods mentioned: EurekAlert! 67, IDW-Online 62. Limitations: 18, 18. Implications: 39, 30. References to other studies: 3, 6. No research: 6, 15.
EurekAlert!: universities + significant publisher/journal presence. IDW-Online: universities + significant non-university research institutes.

Press releases on both platforms dominated by life sciences. Frequently describe methods and practical implications; limitations or references less typical. Press releases are a comparatively homogeneous format of external science communication. Future work: ML to classify larger samples; combine datasets with other external science communication formats such as news articles.
